QF.id: I Clearing Settlement and Risk Management_28

question.text: The ELM for options is calculated on:

answer.text: The notional value of the open short option position
The premium amount of the option
The contract value of the long option position
The intrinsic value of the option

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Options ELM is on the NOTIONAL value of SHORT option positions. For FCY-INR pairs, notional = based on latest FBIL rate. Long option positions don't attract ELM. **The Trap:** For FUTURES, ELM is on MTM value of GROSS open positions. For OPTIONS, it's on notional value of SHORT positions only. **Cross-Exam Context:** Options-specific margin rules are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This means writing (selling) options has higher margin requirements than buying options.
